title: Rotarod (40 RPM and 20 RPM with docking)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"925jk","text":"This protocol describes motor function testing in mice using the rotarod by the Svenningsson lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"t23l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nk4i","text":"Two options for the protocol are included: 1) 40 RPM constant speed after habituation and 2) 20 RPM with docking. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Using a paper tube, transfer each mouse of the same cage onto the rotarod (Ugo Basile, 47650). Always maintain order, mice one into line one, mice 2 into line 2 etc…
Lift the collecting platform and set up the protocol: either max speed of constant 40 RPM or 20 RPM docking.
The time to fall is defined as the moment the mice fall in the receptacle or the moment the mice turn around the rotor more than 5 consecutive times. The maximum trial length is 2 min.
Analyze for each mouse, the time to fall for each session, the average and and max time.
Following habituation, start the rotarod program to reach a speed of 40 RPM within the first 5s of the protocol.

Following habituation, start the rotarod program to reach 20RPM within the first 5 s and proceed for 5 rotations. Then the wheel will stop for 1s and turn towards the opposite direction at 20 RPM and proceed to 5 rotations and repeat that for a maximum of 120 s.
The time to fall is defined as the moment the mice fall in the receptacle or the moment the mice turn around the rotor more than 5 consecutive times. The maximum trial length is 2 min.
The apparatus was thus cleaned thoroughly between each group with 50% ethanol followed by water.
Repeat the test 3 times with at least 6 hr between each trial.
Transfer the cage into the experimental room at least 1 hr before testing.
To avoid stress, always put the cage into a ventilated cage rack.
Wait 2-3 minutes for the mice to habituate to the apparatus.

Repeat the experiment 3 times with at least 6 hr between each trial. If an animal does not fall or turn more than 5 times within 120s of the trial, stop the trial and record “120” as the individual time.

In the 20 RPM docking protocol, most of the animals willfall around 20 to 25s of the first session that corresponds to the first changeof direction. After repeating the protocol (session 2-3), wild type mice shouldbe able to maintain for longer period and turn their body at each revolution.
The time to fall is defined as the moment the mice fall in the receptacle or the moment the mice turn around the rotor more than 5 consecutive times. The maximum trial length is 2 min.
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
